tasks.title: Connection resilience for shared GitNexus client (F3.2)
tasks.notes: Cleared rejected connectionPromise for retry; transport+client onclose/onerror reset sharedClient/connectionPromise; all 4 methods route through single guarded callGitNexus helper returning structured offline result (isError) and resetting state. Added 3 F3.2 resilience tests (vi.resetModules + dynamic import). Build clean; code-intelligence suite 10/10 green. Note: 2 pre-existing time-utils failures are timezone-dependent (IST machine), unrelated to this change.

tasks.title: Pre-flight index check with actionable error (F3.1)
tasks.notes: Added missingIndexResult() + pre-flight fs.existsSync check on <repo>/.gitnexus/meta.json inside the shared callGitNexus helper (runs before getGitNexusClient() is awaited, so a missing index never spawns the child). Calls without a repo param, or with an empty-string repo, forward untouched. Message matches requirements verbatim. Added 5 tests in tests/code-intelligence.test.ts (temp dir via mkdtempSync per provider method asserting exact error text + mockConnect/mockCallTool not called, plus one forwarding test) -- 15/15 green in that file. Build clean.

tasks.title: Code intelligence health in fleet_status (F3.3)
tasks.notes: Added codeIntelligenceHealth(repoDir) + codeIntelligenceCompactLine() to src/tools/check-status.ts. Reads <cwd>/.gitnexus/meta.json read-only (fs.existsSync/readFileSync, no MCP spawn, no network); compares meta.lastCommit to git rev-parse HEAD (execFileSync with 3s timeout) via git rev-list --count for the behind-count. Wired into fleetStatus(): codeIntelligence key in json payload, extra compact line ('code-intel: index present | N nodes / N edges / N files | indexed <ts> | matching HEAD' or '... N commits behind HEAD' or degraded '... HEAD comparison unavailable'; 'code-intel: no index (...)' when absent). All paths caught internally plus a defensive try/catch at the call site -- never throws, never fails fleet_status. New tests/fleet-status-code-intelligence.test.ts (9 tests: absent index, git-unavailable degrade, stats parsing, invalid JSON, missing dir, compact-line rendering for matching/behind/unavailable/absent) -- all green; existing tests/fleet-status-branch.test.ts (6 tests) still green with the new line present. Build clean.

tasks.title: VERIFY Phase 1 -- build, tests, gitnexus analyze, push
tasks.notes: build: npm run build (tsc) clean, no errors. lint: not configured in package.json -- skipped as expected. test: npm test full suite -- 1647 passed, 2 failed, 14 skipped across 115 files; the 2 failures are the known pre-existing tests/time-utils.test.ts timezone-dependent failures (IST machine, beads yashr-302), unrelated to this sprint -- treated as green per instructions. gitnexus analyze: npx gitnexus analyze ran after tests passed and succeeded (non-fatal step regardless) -- incremental update (changed=32 added=30 deleted=19), 4039 nodes / 10951 edges / 214 clusters / 300 flows, ~26.7s. Pushed feat/code-intelligence-abstraction to origin. No PR opened; never pushed to main.

tasks.title: Freshness note + pure comparison function (F2.2)
tasks.notes: Added freshnessNote(lastCommit, head) pure function in new src/tools/code-intelligence-freshness.ts (separate module, avoids circular import between code-intelligence.ts and code-intelligence-gitnexus.ts). Wired into the shared callGitNexus helper: after a successful callTool when params.repo is set and the F3.1 pre-flight passed, reads <repo>/.gitnexus/meta.json.lastCommit and git rev-parse HEAD (execFileSync, 3s timeout, same style as check-status.ts), appends the verbatim note as an extra text content block when they differ via appendFreshnessNote(). All reads wrapped in try/catch returning null on any failure -- never blocks or fails the call; note appended at most once. Tests: new tests/code-intelligence-freshness.test.ts (7 tests: match->null, differ->exact string with 8-char truncation, undefined lastCommit/head/both->null, short SHAs no crash, short-SHA match->null). Added 4 wiring tests to tests/code-intelligence.test.ts (mocking child_process.execFileSync for stubbed HEAD): note appended when differ, no note when match, no note when git throws, no note when meta.json invalid JSON -- code-intelligence.test.ts now 19/19 green (up from 15), full suite 1658 passed / 2 failed (known pre-existing tests/time-utils.test.ts timezone failures, beads yashr-302) / 14 skipped. Build clean.

tasks.title: Re-index at VERIFY checkpoints in PM docs (F2.1)
tasks.notes: Extended VERIFY checkpoint sentence in doer template (skills/pm/doer-reviewer-loop.md lines 166-168) to include npx gitnexus analyze after build, linter, full test suite, explicitly stating non-fatal (if fails, record and continue). Added new bullet to skills/pm/index.md 'When to run' section documenting that VERIFY checkpoints automatically re-run gitnexus analyze incrementally to keep mid-sprint symbols visible to later phases. Both files touched only; no unrelated text reflowed. ASCII only.

tasks.title: Routing guidance in tool descriptions (F1.1)
tasks.notes: Appended routing sentence to all four code_* tool descriptions in src/index.ts (lines 310-325): code_graph, code_impact, code_query, code_context. Each description now includes: 'Prefer this over Glob/Grep/file reads for structural questions (symbol lookup, call chains, impact) -- the answer is pre-indexed.' All descriptions updated with identical appended text. Build clean (no build run yet; T3.4 will verify).

Paragraph includes: kb_session_prime call at session start with hints from diff; kb_query for unfamiliar files with CONFIRMED/INFERRED trust; code_impact for impact questions; guidance to use code_graph/code_impact/code_query/code_context for structural questions, never Glob/Grep. Adapted from doer template style for review work. Other templates byte-identical.
